sections.paragraph:
Κρόκος, ου, ὁ (not a very common name; in the Gk. form e.g. OGI 140; CIG add. 4716d, 44; Κυπρ. I p. 100 no. 74; PLond II, 257, 221; 223 p. 26 [94 a.d.]; BGU 90, 1; 537, 1) Crocus, an Ephesian Christian: θεοῦ ἄξιος καὶ ὑμῶν IEph 2:1. τὸ ποθητόν μοι ὄνομα a person dear to me IRo 10:1.

Gk. Gk. = Greek

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

add. add. = additional

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 570
